page: Amazon Web Services x1e.32xlarge is a memory-intensive server powered by the Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture under a Xen hypervisor, this server delivers 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz and a 45 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration features a massive memory capacity of 3904.0 GB, providing 30.5 GB of RAM per physical core. Additionally, it includes 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. No GPUs are present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core or memory-bound operations. Single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance is average, and memory bandwidth tests show strong results in the top 25% for both small-block cached reads (3825.21 GB/sec) and large-block uncached reads (201.38 GB/sec) and writes (99.14 GB/sec). Ray tracing and extended instruction tests also perform in the top 25%, while database operations and Redis SET operations maintain average throughput.

Given its massive memory footprint and local NVMe SSD storage, the x1e.32xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads. While single-threaded CPU efficiency is low, the sheer volume of RAM and strong memory bandwidth make it highly suitable for large in-memory databases, enterprise-grade SAP HANA deployments, big data processing, and heavy caching layers.
description: Amazon Web Services x1e.32xlarge is a memory-intensive server designed for large-scale, memory-bound enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores, it features an extraordinary 3904.0 GB of RAM alongside 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under a Xen hypervisor, its performance profile is characterized by strong memory bandwidth in the top 25% (reaching 3825.21 GB/sec for cached reads) and average multi-core processing capabilities, despite weak single-core benchmark results. This high resource density makes it highly suitable for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x1e.32xlarge is a memory-intensive server featuring 128 vCPUs, 3904 GB of RAM, and 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x1e.32xlarge is a memory-intensive server with 128 vCPUs, 3.9 TB of RAM, and 3.8 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring nearly four terabytes of RAM and local NVMe storage for massive in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
Massive 3904.0 GB of system memory providing 30.5 GB per core
3840 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong memory bandwidth performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
Best suited for large in-memory databases and enterprise caching layers
